context: Tests whether the agent correctly analyzes three TypeScript modules (all embedded inline in the task) for deepening opportunities. The agent should identify both shallow modules (workspaceRepository with 8 thin DB-wrapping methods, and ledgerFormatter with 6 trivially-thin formatting functions), avoid flagging the already-deep module (lineItemValidator, which hides 6 validation rules behind a single call), propose concrete refactors, use the domain vocabulary from the embedded glossary (Ledger, LineItem, Workspace), and apply the deep-module vocabulary (Module, Interface, Shallow, Deep, Seam, Leverage, Locality).

checklist.name: Identifies workspaceRepository as a shallow module
checklist.description: The agent flags workspaceRepository (or describes it using the canonical Workspace term) as a shallow module. Any mention that its 8 methods each wrap a single DB call, or that callers must know which method to call, counts. Partial credit for identifying it as a candidate without explaining why.

checklist.name: Identifies ledgerFormatter as a shallow module
checklist.description: The agent flags ledgerFormatter (or names it using the Ledger / LineItem terms) as a shallow module. Any mention that its 6 functions are trivial one-liners, or that callers import and call each one individually, counts. Partial credit for identifying it as a candidate without explaining why.

checklist.name: Does NOT flag lineItemValidator as a candidate
checklist.description: The agent correctly avoids flagging lineItemValidator as a shallow module. lineItemValidator exposes one method that hides six distinct validation rules and error aggregation — it is already deep. Award full marks if lineItemValidator is absent from candidates or explicitly called out as already deep. Award 0 if it is listed as a candidate for deepening.

checklist.name: Proposes a specific refactor for at least one candidate
checklist.description: For at least one candidate the agent names a concrete change — not vague advice like 'consolidate this module'. Examples: collapsing workspaceRepository's 8 methods into find/save, or collapsing ledgerFormatter's 6 functions into one formatter call. Award full marks for concrete before/after method counts for both candidates. Award partial marks for a concrete refactor for one candidate.

checklist.name: Proposed refactors reduce interface complexity
checklist.description: Each proposed refactor results in a smaller public interface (fewer exported names or fewer required call sites). The agent articulates the reduction: e.g. '8 methods → 2' or '6 exports → 1 formatter call'. Partial credit for naming a reduction without quantifying it.

checklist.name: Uses deep-module vocabulary
checklist.description: The agent uses at least two of the skill's canonical terms — Module, Interface, Shallow, Deep, Seam, Leverage, Locality — when describing candidates and refactors. Award full marks for three or more terms. Award partial marks for one or two. Award 0 only if none are used.

checklist.name: Applies the deletion test
checklist.description: For at least one candidate, the agent explicitly applies the deletion test: would deleting this module concentrate complexity in its callers, or just move it? The answer should be 'concentrates' — confirming the module is shallow. Award full marks for clear deletion-test reasoning. Award partial marks for mentioning the concept without fully applying the logic.

checklist.name: Uses domain vocabulary from the embedded glossary
checklist.description: The response uses at least one of the domain terms from the embedded glossary: Ledger, LineItem, Workspace. Award full marks for using two or more. Award partial for one. Award 0 only if none are used.
